description_long: This is a robust, industrial-style parcel locker unit constructed primarily from metal, featuring a grid of twelve individual compartments, each with its own numbered door. The front face displays bold red numbers (41-48) and a section label in Cyrillic script, suggesting use in a Russian-speaking region. Each compartment door is equipped with a small handle and a set of indicator lights or buttons, likely for access control or status indication. The top of the unit has a protruding canopy with mounting brackets and a prominent green button or indicator light on the right side. The sides and back are plain, with visible panel seams and a matte, slightly worn finish. The overall design is utilitarian, emphasizing security and durability for outdoor or semi-outdoor urban environments.
description: A heavy-duty metal parcel locker with twelve numbered compartments, red labeling, and a green indicator on top. The front features individual doors with handles and control panels, while the sides and back are plain and industrial.
description_view_0: Front view showing twelve numbered compartment doors, red section label, handles, control panels, and a green indicator on the top right.
description_view_1: Left side view showing the plain, solid metal side panel and the protruding top canopy with the green indicator visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a flat, unadorned metal back with visible seams and no interactive features.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, with a plain metal surface and the top canopy extending outward.
attribute_access_door_design: Swing-out metal doors with small recessed handles and possible indicator lights or buttons for each compartment.
attribute_location_signage: Red section label in Cyrillic script ('СЕКЦИЯ №9') and red compartment numbers (41-48) on the front.
